context: Tests whether the agent understands deep vs shallow modules and can redesign a shallow module into a deep one by reducing the number of public methods, simplifying parameters, and hiding complexity behind the interface.

checklist.name: Fewer public methods
checklist.description: configLoader-v2.js exports fewer public methods than the original 7 — ideally 3 or fewer (e.g. load, get, getOptional)

checklist.name: Simpler parameters
checklist.description: Callers no longer need to pass the raw config object between function calls — the module manages config state internally or via a returned accessor object

checklist.name: Complexity hidden
checklist.description: Type checking, validation, and error messaging logic is hidden inside the module — callers do not need to call separate getStringValue / getNumberValue / getBooleanValue functions

checklist.name: Design rationale explains shallowness
checklist.description: design-rationale.md explicitly names what made the original module shallow (e.g. large interface that passes complexity to callers)

checklist.name: Design rationale explains deepening decisions
checklist.description: design-rationale.md explains at least 2 specific interface decisions made to deepen the module

checklist.name: Method count comparison
checklist.description: design-rationale.md explicitly compares the number of public methods before and after

checklist.name: Tests on new public interface only
checklist.description: configLoader-v2.test.js only tests through the new public interface — no tests on extracted private helpers or internal functions

checklist.name: Behavior preserved
checklist.description: New module supports the same operations as the original (required keys, optional keys with defaults, type validation) — no functionality dropped

checklist.name: Deletion test applied
checklist.description: design-rationale.md mentions that deleting the original module would scatter complexity to many callers (deletion test reasoning)

checklist.name: Can reduce methods check
checklist.description: design-rationale.md explicitly asks 'can I reduce the number of methods?' and answers it for the original module
